page: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a virtual machine in the High Frequency Compute family. It features 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by an Intel Core Processor (Skylake, IBRS) running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server is configured with shared CPU allocation and 1.0 thread per core. It includes 58.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 3.62 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 1024 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates average overall performance across multiple compute and memory workloads. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests scored 1884.79 and 14058.95 respectively, placing them in the average category. Single-threaded gzip compression demonstrated strong performance at 30.89 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth tests reveal a notable bottleneck; while cached small-block reads achieved an average of 907.89 GB/sec, large-block uncached reads (24.85 GB/sec) and writes (13.66 GB/sec) fell into the weak and poor categories. Additionally, the static web serving throughput of 6.08 GB/sec and the PassMark CPU physics score of 731.79 are classified as weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server offers a high-density resource profile with a substantial 1024 GB NVMe SSD and 58.0 GB of RAM, making it a viable option for workloads requiring local storage capacity without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. Given the average database operations score of 5215.72 and Redis SET throughput of 11.03 million operations per second, this instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, medium-sized databases, and caching layers that are not heavily constrained by uncached memory bandwidth or high-throughput web serving.
description: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute virtual server featuring 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on an Intel Skylake x86_64 architecture with shared CPU allocation. It is equipped with 58.0 GB of memory and a bundled 1024 GB NVMe SSD. Performance benchmarks show average results for CPU-bound tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 14058.95 and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 30.89 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is weak to poor, and static web serving throughput is limited to 6.08 GB/sec. This server provides a cost-effective balance of high storage capacity and moderate compute power, making it suitable for general-purpose applications, caching layers, and database operations that do not require dedicated CPU resources or extreme memory throughput.
og_description: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute server with 16 Intel vCPUs, 58 GB RAM, and a 1024 GB NVMe SSD, offering balanced general-purpose performance with average database and CPU benchmarks.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-16c-58gb is a High Frequency Compute server featuring 16 Intel vCPUs, 58 GB RAM, and a 1024 GB NVMe SSD for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual server combining sixteen shared Intel cores, fifty-eight gigabytes of memory, and one terabyte of fast NVMe storage.
bullet_points: 16 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs and 16 physical cores
58.0 GB of system memory and 1024 GB local NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance of 30.89 GB/sec
Average database operations and Redis SET performance
Weak uncached memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput
Suited for general-purpose applications, caching, and database workloads
